title: Prunus serrulata luteoides
taxonomy: Prunus serrulata luteoides is a cultivar of Prunus serrulata, commonly known as the Japanese cherry or sakura. This ornamental tree belongs to the family Rosaceae. Within the genus Prunus, Prunus serrulata luteoides is classified as a subspecies or cultivar of Prunus serrulata, a species native to East Asia. The classification places it within the subfamily Amygdaloideae and the order Rosales. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Prunus serrulata luteoides from other Prunus species and cultivars.
uses: Prunus serrulata luteoides is known for its traditional medicinal uses in East Asian cultures. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of infections and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal remedies to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. Additionally, it is said to have antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Prunus serrulata luteoides for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Prunus serrulata luteoides, also known as the Japanese yellow cherry, is a species of flowering cherry tree. While there is limited information available on the health benefits of Prunus serrulata luteoides specifically, its leaves and flowers are traditionally used in herbal medicine in Japan. The plant is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Prunus serrulata luteoides.

parts: Prunus serrulata luteoides flowers, leaves, and bark are used in traditional medicine, primarily in East Asia. The flowers are used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The leaves and bark are used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, it is used as a treatment for certain types of wounds and ulcers.
preparations: Prunus serrulata luteoides is a type of flowering cherry tree. Herbal preparations derived from Prunus serrulata luteoides are primarily used in traditional medicine in East Asia.
